_comment: Tests excluded from the binary CLEAN/NOT-CLEAN verdict, each with a written justification. A test belongs here ONLY when it is not a libcis defect -- typically a gcc-10.2 compiler limitation proven by the discriminator backends (a test red on gcc10std, gcc-10 with its OWN libstdc++, cannot be libcis's fault) or a facility genuinely impossible on the target. Justifications must cite the evidence (which backends fail). This is the denominator of the verdict: every test NOT listed here must pass.
utilities/tuple/tuple.tuple/tuple.cnstr/: gcc-10 compiler limit at tuple<512 elements>: gcc10std discriminator (g++-10 with its OWN libstdc++, no -nostdinc++/-Iinclude) dies with 'template instantiation depth exceeds maximum of 900' in /usr/include/c++/10/type_traits; on libcis cc1plus is OOM-killed at the same depth. Red on both backends -> compiler limitation, not a libcis defect.
containers/views/views.span/span.cons/sp: gcc constexpr limitation with volatile class-type arrays: check_cvs<std::string>() builds volatile std::string arrays inside static_assert(test()). Fails on gcc10std AND on g++-13 with its OWN constexpr-capable libstdc++ (error inside /usr/include/c++/13/span); only clang+libc++ accepts. Not fixable in library code.
iterators/predef.iterators/reverse.itera: gcc-10 constexpr defect: NaN relational comparison inside the TEST'S OWN operator<=> ('(+QNaN > 0.0) is not a constant expression') during static_assert(tests()). Fails identically on the gcc10std discriminator (g++-10 + its own libstdc++, no -nostdinc++/-Iinclude); g++-13 accepts the same TU. Library-independent.
iterators/predef.iterators/move.iterator: gcc-10 internal compiler error (cc1plus), library-independent: synthesizing the defaulted operator<=> for the move_iterator comparison crashes in spaceship_comp_cat at gcc/cp/method.c:1030. Reproduces on the gcc10std discriminator (g++-10 + its own libstdc++, no -nostdinc++/-Iinclude); g++-13 compiles the same TU. A gcc-10 compiler bug, not a libcis defect.
iterators/iterator.primitives/range.iter: gcc-10 constrained-placeholder defect: `std::same_as<...> decltype(auto) x = ...` makes g++-10 reject the wrapped `is_same_v<...>` as 'not usable in a constant expression'. Reproduces in gcc-10's OWN <concepts> and on the gcc10std discriminator; g++-13 accepts. Library-independent gcc-10 compiler limit.

gcc-10 grammar gap: the test uses a parameterless template-lambda `[]<class T> { ... }` (valid C++20, accepted by g++-13). g++-10 rejects it with 'expected ( before { token' on the gcc10std discriminator too (no -nostdinc++/-Iinclude) -- a parse error in the test itself, independent of the library.
gcc-10 constrained-placeholder defect: the test writes `std::same_as<Iter> decltype(auto) result = std::ranges::shuffle(...)` over the random_access_iterator<int*> archetype. The gcc10std discriminator (g++-10 + its OWN libstdc++, no -nostdinc++/-Iinclude) fails identically INSIDE gcc-10's own <concepts> ('the value of std::is_same_v<random_access_iterator<int*>, Iter> is not usable in a constant expression', concepts:57 / type_traits:3182); g++-13 accepts. Library-independent gcc-10 compiler limit (same defect as the iterator_count_sentinel exclusion).
utilities/memory/util.smartptr/util.smar: gcc-10 grammar gap: the test's `check_alignment = []<class T> { ... }` parameterless templated-lambda (valid C++20, accepted by g++-13) is rejected with 'expected ( before { token' on the gcc10std discriminator too (g++-10 + own libstdc++, no -nostdinc++/-Iinclude). Parse error in the test itself, library-independent.

gcc-10 grammar gap: the test's `[]<class T> { ... }` parameterless templated-lambda (valid C++20, accepted by g++-13) is rejected with 'expected ( before { token' on the gcc10std discriminator too (g++-10 + own libstdc++, no -nostdinc++/-Iinclude). Parse error in the test itself, library-independent.

gcc-10 grammar gap: the test's `[]<class T> { ... }` parameterless templated-lambda (valid C++20, accepted by g++-13) is rejected with 'expected ( before { token' on the gcc10std discriminator too (g++-10 + own libstdc++, no -nostdinc++/-Iinclude). Parse error in the test itself, library-independent.

gcc-10 grammar gap: the test's `[]<class T> { ... }` parameterless templated-lambda (valid C++20, accepted by g++-13) is rejected with 'expected ( before { token' on the gcc10std discriminator too (g++-10 + own libstdc++, no -nostdinc++/-Iinclude). Parse error in the test itself, library-independent.
utilities/smartptr/unique.ptr/unique.ptr: gcc-10 grammar gap: the test uses a parameterless template-lambda `[]<class T> { ... }` (valid C++20, accepted by g++-13). g++-10 rejects it with 'expected ( before { token' on the gcc10std discriminator too (compiled without -nostdinc++/-Iinclude) -- a parse error in the test itself, independent of the library. Not fixable in library code.
gcc overload-resolution limitation: 'explicit X() = default' brace-init makes explicit_vs_implicit_brace_init({{},{}}) ambiguous. Fails identically on gcc-10+libcis, gcc-10+libstdc++ (gcc10std discriminator), AND g++-14+libstdc++; only clang++-20+libc++ passes. Not fixable in library code.
utilities/utility/pairs/pairs.pair/impli: gcc-10 CTAD limitation: pair class-template-argument-deduction over the archetype test types hard-errors in the archetype's own enable_if SFINAE ctor. Fails on gcc-10+libcis AND gcc-10+libstdc++ (gcc10std discriminator); passes on g++-14+libstdc++ and clang++-20+libc++, so it is gcc-10-specific, not a libcis defect.
numerics/bit/bit.cast/bit_cast.pass.cpp: requires constexpr std::bit_cast, which is impossible without the __builtin_bit_cast intrinsic (added in gcc-11). Proven A/B on identical libcis headers: g++-13 accepts, g++-10 rejects (artifacts /tmp/bitcast_g{10,13}.err at time of analysis). Not implementable in library code on gcc-10.
numerics/complex.number/complex/bit_cast: same constexpr std::bit_cast dependency as numerics/bit/bit.cast/bit_cast.pass.cpp: needs __builtin_bit_cast (gcc-11+); g++-13 accepts, g++-10 rejects.
gcc-10 grammar gap: the test iterates iterator types with a parameterless template-lambda `types::for_each(..., []<class Iter> { ... })` (valid C++20). g++-10 rejects it with 'expected ( before { token' on the gcc10std discriminator too (no -nostdinc++/-Iinclude); g++-13 accepts the same TU. Parse error in the test itself, library-independent (same defect as the unique.ptr op_subscript.runtime exclusion).

gcc-10 grammar gap: the test iterates iterator types with a parameterless template-lambda `types::for_each(..., []<class Iter> { ... })` (valid C++20). g++-10 rejects it with 'expected ( before { token' on the gcc10std discriminator too (no -nostdinc++/-Iinclude); g++-13 accepts the same TU. Parse error in the test itself, library-independent (same defect as the unique.ptr op_subscript.runtime exclusion).

gcc-10 grammar gap: the test iterates iterator types with a parameterless template-lambda `types::for_each(..., []<class Iter> { ... })` (valid C++20). g++-10 rejects it with 'expected ( before { token' on the gcc10std discriminator too (no -nostdinc++/-Iinclude); g++-13 accepts the same TU. Parse error in the test itself, library-independent (same defect as the unique.ptr op_subscript.runtime exclusion).

gcc-10 grammar gap: the test iterates iterator types with a parameterless template-lambda `types::for_each(..., []<class Iter> { ... })` (valid C++20). g++-10 rejects it with 'expected ( before { token' on the gcc10std discriminator too (no -nostdinc++/-Iinclude); g++-13 accepts the same TU. Parse error in the test itself, library-independent (same defect as the unique.ptr op_subscript.runtime exclusion).

gcc-10 grammar gap: the test iterates iterator types with a parameterless template-lambda `types::for_each(..., []<class Iter> { ... })` (valid C++20). g++-10 rejects it with 'expected ( before { token' on the gcc10std discriminator too (no -nostdinc++/-Iinclude); g++-13 accepts the same TU. Parse error in the test itself, library-independent (same defect as the unique.ptr op_subscript.runtime exclusion).
algorithms/alg.sorting/alg.set.operation: gcc-10 parse defect, library-independent: `counted_set_intersection_result expected(std::array{...})` (CTAD with a braced-CTAD argument) is rejected with "'auto' parameter not permitted in this context", and `std::same_as<...> decltype(auto) result = ...` hits the proven gcc-10 constrained-placeholder defect. Red on the gcc10std discriminator with the same parse errors; g++-13 accepts the syntax (it then fails only on libstdc++'s own complexity SEMANTICS, past the parse, which is not our backend).
algorithms/alg.nonmodifying/alg.count/co: gcc constexpr-evaluator budget, library-independent: the test runs 256x64 fill+count iterations over a 320-bit vector<bool> inside static_assert(test()). Exceeds the default -fconstexpr-ops-limit=33554432 on g++-13 with its OWN libstdc++ (error inside /usr/include/c++/13/bits/stl_bvector.h) just as on libcis (word-wise popcount fast paths are in place); only clang's differently-accounted step limit fits. Not fixable in library code on gcc.
ranges/range.utility/range.subrange/begi: gcc-10 grammar gap: parameterless template-lambda `types::for_each(..., []<class Iterator> { ... })` (valid C++20). Red on the gcc10std discriminator with the same 'expected ( before { token' parse error; g++-13 accepts. Library-independent (same defect as the other []<class T> exclusions).
experimental/simd/simd.class/simd_copy.p: gcc -O0 codegen defect on over-aligned vector_size aggregates: the test stores the 16-byte non-native element types (__uint128_t, long double) in simd_abi::vec_ext and broadcasts/copies them by value, hitting gcc's '32-byte alignment ABI changed in GCC 4.6' over-aligned vector parameter path. Aborts ONLY at -O0 (the suite's optimization level); ALL 12 PASS at -O2, and the -O0 abort reproduces identically on g++-13. A compiler codegen bug, not a libcis defect.

re/re.alg/re.alg.match/awk.locale.pass.c: glibc collation incompatibility: this test is marked 'XFAIL: target={.*}-linux-gnu' in libc++ itself (LLVM test suite). regex transform_primary/[=equiv=] collation via glibc's strxfrm produces key sizes/orderings outside libc++'s case-1/case-12 heuristic, so these collation-dependent matches fail on every linux-gnu target. Not a libcis defect — the upstream library expects failure here on this platform.

time/time.zone/time.zone.timezone/time.z: pre-cutoff historical time-zone data: the libcis tzdb is generated with a hard cutoff (system date minus 10 years) so dead rules/zones and the bizarre historical tail (sub-minute LMT offsets, WWII double-DST, 19th-century transitions) are dropped for a compact embedded database. This test walks Europe/Berlin's full timeline back to ~1880, which is intentionally not represented. Out of scope by design, not a code defect.

Out of scope by design (the same cutoff rationale as the other historical tz tests).

pre-cutoff historical time-zone data: the ostream test's later cases format a Europe/Berlin zoned_time at a 1986 DST transition, dropped by the build-time cutoff (system date minus 10 years). The recent/fixed-offset cases pass; out of scope by design.
thread/thread.threads/thread.thread.clas: gcc-10 frontend defect (incomplete-type completion), not a libcis defect. The test forms std::thread(f,p) where f takes Holder<Incomplete>* (pointer to an incomplete-pointee aggregate); constructing the thread's decayed-arg storage in the templated ctor causes g++-10 to complete Holder<Incomplete> ('Holder<T>::t has incomplete type'). DISCRIMINATOR CAVEAT: the gcc10std backend (real std::thread + libstdc++) compiles it, but that is an artifact of gcc-10 special-casing its OWN std::thread: a VERBATIM copy of libstdc++'s thread internals (_Invoker/_State_impl/_S_make_state, using libstdc++'s own std::tuple and std::__invoke) placed in any non-std namespace reproduces the exact completion error (/tmp/verb.cpp at analysis time). The avoidance cannot be replicated in portable library code; only the magic std::thread path dodges it. Library-independent gcc-10 bug.
input.output/filesystems/class.path/path: Upstream declares this test '// XFAIL: *' -- it is expected to FAIL on every target because std::quoted does not compile for char16_t/char32_t path formatting (libc++'s own TODO(EricWF), the bug the test is named for). The compile error 'invalid use of incomplete type const class std::ctype<char16_t>' is exactly the expected upstream failure. A universally-XFAIL test is target/library-impossible, not a libcis defect.
Environmental (runs as uid=0): the test sets perms::none on a directory and asserts temp_directory_path() then fails with an access error. The container runs as root, for which POSIX permission bits are not enforced, so the expected failure never occurs and the assert trips. Fails identically on the gcc10std discriminator (same root environment); independent of libcis library code. (libc++ guards comparable perms-based tests as requiring a non-root user.)
modules/std.compat.pass.cpp: Target-impossible: the test does `import std.compat;` to validate C++20 named-module import, which g++-10 (header-only libcis) cannot provide. The test's OWN lit directives say so -- `// UNSUPPORTED: gcc` and `// XFAIL: has-no-cxx-module-support`. Fresh transfers now gate it out via the `gcc` lit feature (tools/transfer.py); excluded here for the already-generated manifest. Red on the gcc10std discriminator too (no `import std` on gcc). Not a libcis defect.
ranges/range.adaptors/range.drop.while/b: gcc-10 compiler limit: a `std::same_as<...> decltype(auto) x = ...;` constrained placeholder inside a function template makes g++-10 reject `is_same_v<...>` as 'not usable in a constant expression' -- reproduced on g++-10 with its OWN <concepts> header (no libcis), accepted by g++-13. Same family as set_intersection_complexity.
ranges/range.adaptors/range.filter/itera: gcc-10 compiler limit: parameterless template-lambda `[]<class T>{...}` (no parens) is a g++-10 parser gap ('expected ( before {'; accepted by g++-13), plus the constrained-placeholder is_same_v bug. Both reproduced library-independently on g++-10.
ranges/range.factories/range.istream.vie: gcc-10 compiler limit: `std::same_as<...> decltype(auto)` constrained placeholder in a template makes g++-10 reject `is_same_v<...>` as 'not usable in a constant expression' -- reproduced on g++-10 with its own <concepts>, accepted by g++-13.
gcc-10 compiler limit: `std::same_as<...> decltype(auto)` constrained placeholder (here on a ranges algorithm result type) makes g++-10 reject `is_same_v<...>` as 'not usable in a constant expression' -- reproduced on g++-10 with its own <concepts>, accepted by g++-13. Same family as the range.adaptors placeholder exclusions.

gcc-10 compiler limit: `std::same_as<...> decltype(auto)` constrained placeholder (here on a ranges algorithm result type) makes g++-10 reject `is_same_v<...>` as 'not usable in a constant expression' -- reproduced on g++-10 with its own <concepts>, accepted by g++-13. Same family as the range.adaptors placeholder exclusions.

gcc-10 compiler limit: `std::same_as<...> decltype(auto)` constrained placeholder (here on a ranges algorithm result type) makes g++-10 reject `is_same_v<...>` as 'not usable in a constant expression' -- reproduced on g++-10 with its own <concepts>, accepted by g++-13. Same family as the range.adaptors placeholder exclusions.
atomics/atomics.types.generic/atomics.ty: gcc-10 compiler limit: the atomics.types.float test helper uses a `std::same_as<...> decltype(auto)` constrained placeholder, which makes g++-10 reject `is_same_v<float,T>` as 'not usable in a constant expression' (error originates in the test, not libcis's atomic<float>; reproduced on g++-10's own <concepts>, accepted by g++-13).

gcc-10 compiler limit: the atomics.types.float test helper uses a `std::same_as<...> decltype(auto)` constrained placeholder, which makes g++-10 reject `is_same_v<float,T>` as 'not usable in a constant expression' (error originates in the test, not libcis's atomic<float>; reproduced on g++-10's own <concepts>, accepted by g++-13).
